abstract:
In this meeting, the major issues of arid and semi-arid areas regarding
water scarcity, nutrient deficiency, salinity, alkalinity, small and marginal
land holdings, poor economic status, increasing food prices and high
population growth were discussed. Scientists suggested management
strategies to overcome these problems and to enhance agricultural
productivity. Moreover, it was emphasized that vast untapped potential
exists in rainy season fallow and rice fallow lands in India which could
be harnessed by adopting improved technologies.
funders: Government of India - Ministry of Agriculture
Government of India - Ministry of Rural Development
publisher: International Crops Research Institute for the Semi-Arid Tropics
note: We sincerely thank Dr SS chahal, Vice Chancellor, Maharana Pratap University of Agriculture and technology (MPUAT), Udaipur, Rajasthan, for hosting the workshop at MPUAT, Udaipur. We are grateful Dr. Pratap Singh, Director of research, MPUAT for the support. we acknowledges the help of Dr. Chauracia and his team from BAIF, Udaipur, for providing logistical support and making arrangement of the workshop. We thank the help of M/s Iqbal Hussain of MPUAT, and TP Mathur and CK Pal of ICRISAT for their timely support and help in organizing the workshop. We gatefully acknowledge the financial support provided by Sir Dorabji Tata Trust, Sir Ratan Tata Trust, Ministry of Agriculture and Ministry of Rural Development, and Government of India for funding the projects to ICRISAT-led consortium. We thank Ms N Shalini for language editing; Mr KNV Satya Narayana for Administrative support and communication office ICRISAT for production of this report.
title: Productivity Enhancement through Sustainable Management of Natural Resources: Proceedings of Tata-ICRISAT-ICAR and Model Watershed Projects’Review and Planning Meeting 4-6 May 2010
